statement.markdown: Write a function that takes a CSS string as input and returns the minimum width required for a media query to be applied. The media query should match devices with a screen width greater than or equal to the specified width. If the media query is not found, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing CSS rules, e.g. '@media (min-width: 500px) { body { background-color: #f2f2f2; } }'
statement.outputFormat: The minimum width required for the media query to be applied, or -1 if not found
statement.notes: Assume that the input CSS string is well-formed and contains at most one media query.

constraints.description: Minimum width for the media query

examples.explanation: The minimum width required for the media query to be applied is 500px
No media query is found in the input CSS string

hints.text: Look for the '@media' keyword in the input CSS string

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Media Query Bugs
editorial.intuition: The media query should match devices with a screen width greater than or equal to the specified width
editorial.bruteForce: You can use regular expressions to find the media query in the input CSS string
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a simple string search to find the media query in the input CSS string
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n), where n is the length of the input CSS string
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where the media query is not found
